title: The Fox and the Crow
lines: A Crow, having stolen a bit of meat, flew up into a tree and perched there with the morsel in her beak.
A Fox, seeing this, longed for the dainty morsel and began to think of a way to get it.
"Good morning, Mistress Crow!" he called out.
"How well you are looking to-day: how glossy your feathers; how bright your eye!"
"I feel sure your voice must surpass that of other birds, just as your beauty does; let me hear one song from you that I may greet you as the Queen of Birds."
The Crow, pleased by these compliments, stretched her neck and opened her beak to sing.
The moment she opened her mouth, the meat fell to the ground, and the Fox picked it up and ran away with it.
"Never mind the meat, Madam," said the Fox, turning back.
"You have lost it, it is true, but you have learned a lesson worth a hundred times more than the meat was worth."
